hyperedges.label: Phase 3 Feature Set (Email, Error Monitoring, Image Optimization)
Bakong KHQR Payment and Verification Flow

nodes.label: Rattanak Coffee E-Commerce Platform
nodes.rationale: Cambodia's cafe scene is booming but small cafe owners lack a reliable centralized supplier; the platform provides a single supplier with transparent pricing, detailed product information, and delivery options for cafe owners, baristas, restaurants, and home enthusiasts.

nodes.label: Bakong KHQR Payment Integration
nodes.rationale: Cambodian QR payment rail chosen for checkout; configured via BAKONG_ACCOUNT_ID, BAKONG_TOKEN, and BAKONG_PROD_BASE_API_URL_MD5 (payment verification endpoint).

nodes.label: Six-Table PostgreSQL Schema (products, profiles, orders, order_items, product_reviews, addresses)
nodes.norm_label: six-table postgresql schema (products, profiles, orders, order_items, product_reviews, addresses)

nodes.label: Row Level Security Policies
nodes.rationale: RLS applied to all six tables so data access control is enforced at the database layer rather than only in application code.

nodes.label: Email Notifications System (Phase 3)
nodes.rationale: Confirmation emails send automatically on POST /api/orders only when the user has a valid email and RESEND_API_KEY is set, keeping email an optional non-blocking side effect of order creation; template uses brand color #5F1B2C and includes tracking number, items, 10% VAT and shipping breakdown.

nodes.label: Error Boundary & Tracking (Phase 3)
nodes.rationale: Development mode shows detailed error info while production hides details behind a friendly message; Sentry integration activates automatically only when available, so error monitoring degrades gracefully without the optional dependency.

nodes.label: Image Optimization Enhancements (Phase 3)
nodes.rationale: next.config.ts configured for AVIF/WebP formats, multiple device sizes, and 30-day cache TTL to reduce bandwidth for responsive product images; blur placeholder support added for perceived performance.

nodes.label: Bakong API Documentation (identical copy of KHQR SDK Documentation v2.7)
nodes.author: National Bank of Cambodia
nodes.norm_label: bakong api documentation (identical copy of khqr sdk documentation v2.7)

nodes.label: KHQR Code Specification
nodes.rationale: Standardizing the QR code specification promotes wider mobile retail payments in Cambodia, gives a consistent merchant/consumer experience, and enables interoperability across schemes, e-wallets, and banks: a single QR receives transactions from any payment provider through Bakong, encouraging small merchants to adopt it. Created for retail/remittance in Cambodia and cross-border.

nodes.label: Prakas KHQR Code Specification in Cambodia
nodes.norm_label: prakas khqr code specification in cambodia

nodes.label: BakongKHQR SDK (iOS, Java, C#, Javascript)
nodes.rationale: Distributed for four platforms (CocoaPods, Maven/Gradle, NuGet, NPM) so any bank or third-party app in the Bakong ecosystem can generate and process KHQR codes with identical semantics; package namespace changed from .org to .gov in v2.5.

nodes.label: Generate KHQR (Individual and Merchant)
nodes.rationale: Produces an EMV QR string plus an md5 hash; a timestamp is embedded in the QR string so each generated code is unique. Individual type requires BakongAccountID and MerchantName; Merchant type additionally requires MerchantID and AcquiringBank.

nodes.label: Verify KHQR
nodes.rationale: Returns CRC validation (valid/invalid) for a KHQR string; v2.3 fixed the verify function to treat QRs containing optional non-standard subtags as valid.

nodes.label: Decode KHQR
nodes.rationale: Extracts all EMV tag fields (bakongAccountID, merchant info, amount, currency, timestamp, CRC, UPI/alternate-language tags) from a KHQR string without validating required fields (since v1.7).

nodes.label: Generate KHQR Deeplink
nodes.rationale: Converts a KHQR string into a short link for app-to-app payment routing; the deeplink API URL is provided by the NBC team, and generated short links differ on every call and depend on which API URL is used.

nodes.label: KHQR Standard Response Format
nodes.rationale: Uniform envelope across all SDK functions and platforms: status {code 0=success/1=failed, errorCode 1-43 with defined messages, message} plus function-specific data; consistent error codes let integrators handle validation failures identically in iOS, Java, C#, and Javascript.

nodes.label: MD5 Hash for Transaction Status Verification
nodes.rationale: The md5 returned alongside the generated QR is the hash value of the Bakong KHQR used to verify transaction status against the Bakong Open API — the mechanism a merchant backend polls to confirm a customer actually paid.
nodes.norm_label: md5 hash for transaction status verification

nodes.norm_label: khmer brand text (កាហវេរតន:)

nodes.norm_label: coffee bean and leaf decorative motif
